name: From Ontinyent to Bocairent via the Camino del Cid
name_original: Desde Ontinyent a Bocairent por el Camino del Cid
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ontinyent traverse a diverse landscape in the Vall d'Albaida region of Valencia, Spain. The area is characterized by multiple mountain ranges, including Serra Grossa and Serra Mariola, offering varied climbs and descents. Cyclists can explore scenic valleys, impressive canyons, and routes along the Clariano River, which features natural pools like Pou Clar. This region provides a network of quiet roads and trails suitable for different fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Ontinyent The most popular touring cycling route is From Ontinyent to Bocairent via the Camino del Cid, a 15.2 miles (24.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects two historic towns through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Water Trail – Cava de la Pedrera Shelter loop from Ontinyent, a difficult 19.1 miles (30.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and explores natural water features and shelters. Local touring cyclists also love the Pou Clar – Mountain Valley View loop from Ontinyent, a 46.1 miles (74.2 km) trail leading through mountain valleys and past the natural pools of Pou Clar, often completed in about 4 hours 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Ontinyent is defined by mountain ranges, natural parks, and scenic valleys, with options for both relaxed rides and challenging circuits. The network includes routes with mostly paved surfaces as well as unpaved segments. The routes in Ontinyent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ontinyent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Ontinyent?
A: Ontinyent, often called the 'Tuscany of Valencia,' offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find quiet roads traversing multiple mountain ranges like Serra Grossa and Serra Mariola, providing varied climbs and descents. The region also features scenic valleys, impressive canyons, and routes along the Clariano River. While many routes have mostly paved surfaces, there are also unpaved segments for those seeking more adventurous rides.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Ontinyent?
A: Yes, Ontinyent offers routes for various skill levels. While many routes involve significant elevation, there are options with mostly paved surfaces that are more accessible. For example, the region's greenways (Vías Verdes) offer accessible, traffic-free paths perfect for family cycling, though specific greenways within Ontinyent are not detailed in the provided data, the Valencian Community generally has them.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Ontinyent pass through stunning natural areas and near historical sites. A major highlight is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;, a series of crystal-clear pools formed by the Clariano River, which you can experience on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19427485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar – Mountain Valley View loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bocairent&lt;/a&gt;, known for its old town, or pass by significant peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Ontinyent is known for its challenging routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17303327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Trail – Cava de la Pedrera Shelter loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.8 km path with substantial climbing. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19427485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar – Mountain Valley View loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 74.2 km with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Ontinyent?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather, making them perfect for exploring the diverse landscapes without the intense heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. The natural parks, like Serra Mariola, are particularly beautiful during these seasons.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Ontinyent?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Ontinyent are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17303327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Trail – Cava de la Pedrera Shelter loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19427485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar – Mountain Valley View loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6902349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ontinyent – Pou d'Olleta loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Ontinyent?
A: The touring cycling routes around Ontinyent are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from quiet roads through mountain ranges to scenic valleys and natural pools. The variety of routes catering to different fitness levels and the stunning natural beauty, including areas like Serra Mariola Natural Park, are frequently highlighted.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in Ontinyent?
A: There are over 70 touring cycling routes available around Ontinyent, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from mountain ranges to scenic valleys and natural parks.

Q: Are there routes that explore the natural parks around Ontinyent?
A: Yes, Ontinyent benefits from its proximity to several natural parks, including Serra Mariola Natural Park and Font Roja Natural Park. Many routes will take you through or near these protected areas, allowing you to experience their rich biodiversity and stunning landscapes. The Sierra de Mariola, in particular, is known for its botanical diversity and mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that connect historical towns or cultural sites?
A: Certainly. The region offers routes that blend natural beauty with cultural heritage. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919485052/from-ontinyent-to-bocairent-via-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Ontinyent to Bocairent via the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt; connects two historic towns, offering a journey through varied terrain and cultural landmarks. Ontinyent itself boasts a lovely old town with an old quarter that dates back centuries.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevations for touring cycling routes in Ontinyent?
A: Routes vary significantly in length and elevation to suit different riders. You can find shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919485052/from-ontinyent-to-bocairent-via-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Ontinyent to Bocairent via the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 24.5 km with 330 meters of elevation gain. For more challenging rides, options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14679247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Trail – Ontinyent loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; cover around 52 km with over 560 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19427485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar – Mountain Valley View loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; extends to 74.2 km with over 1,000 meters of climbing.
tips: Ontinyent is located in the south-west of the province of Valencia on the banks of the Clariano River and is landscaped by the mountainous relief of the Serra Grossa. Ontinyent has a very beautiful old town, in whose winding, narrow streets there is a lot to discover.

El Pou Clar is a river area of significant natural and landscape value. This spot marks the beginning of the Clariano River, which in its first few meters has formed over time a string of pools on calcareous rock.
You can enjoy the pools on hot days to take a refreshing swim, although the place is worth it all year round because there are very nice hiking trails there and it has picnic tables and parking for cars on the side of the road.

As soon as you cycle towards Bocairent, you are presented with an impressive sight. The old town with its branched streets nestles high up on a rocky ridge. Once there, you can push your bike a little while strolling through the alleys, admiring the church and eating delicious food.

Incidentally, the old town was created under Muslim rule, it was only conquered by Spanish troops around 1245 and then became part of the Kingdom of Valencia.
